digest.top_line: KEYNOTE-689 + NIVOPOSTOP: two positive phase 3 IO trials in resectable LA-HNSCC, IO layered onto standard adjuvant cisplatin-CRT with RT unchanged.
digest.tldr: Head/neck carried the day: perioperative pembro (KEYNOTE-689) and post-op nivo (NIVOPOSTOP) both add IO to standard cisplatin-based adjuvant CRT without touching RT dose, fractionation, or target volume. NIVOPOSTOP 3-yr DFS ~63% vs 53%; KEYNOTE-689 EFS positive but magnitude not in source.

sites.intro: Two positive adjuvant IO trials in resectable disease, both leaving the RT backbone intact.

studies.name: KEYNOTE-689 vs NIVOPOSTOP
studies.tldr: Round-up of two positive phase 3 IO trials in resectable LA-HNSCC: perioperative pembro and post-op nivo added to adjuvant CRT.
details: 🔍 Both retain standard adjuvant cisplatin-based (chemo)RT; IO is added, RT dose/fractionation/target volume unchanged

details.text: 📊 KEYNOTE-689 vs NIVOPOSTOP at a glance

details: 💊 KEYNOTE-689: 2 cycles neoadjuvant pembro → surgery → risk-adapted adjuvant RT/CRT + pembro → maintenance pembro

details.text: 📊 NIVOPOSTOP high-risk criteria for postop CRT + nivo

details: 🔗 KEYNOTE-689 = first positive perioperative IO trial in resectable HNSCC in >2 decades (per source)
⚠️ Educational round-up, not primary data; KEYNOTE-689 EFS magnitude not reported in source; NIVOPOSTOP is ASCO 2025 abstract-level
studies.significance: Neither trial changes the RT prescription: both keep standard adjuvant cisplatin-CRT. What moves for the radonc reader is IO placement around that RT course, neoadjuvant pembro before surgery (KEYNOTE-689) versus nivo added to postop CRT (NIVOPOSTOP, 3-yr DFS ~63% vs 53%). The role is integrating IO into unchanged local therapy, not re-planning it.
studies.monday_clinic: In resectable stage III-IVA LA-HNSCC these inform two settings: perioperative pembro for newly-diagnosed resectable disease (KEYNOTE-689), and nivo on postop cisplatin-CRT for high-risk resected pts with extranodal extension or positive margins (NIVOPOSTOP); the RT backbone is unchanged in both.
open_questions: Optimal IO sequencing: perioperative vs purely postoperative
Can adjuvant CRT be de-escalated when IO is added

related_trials.brief_title: Sintilimab Plus Chemotherapy as Neoadjuvant and Adjuvant Treatment for Locally Advanced Oral Squamous Cell Carcinoma
related_trials.brief_summary:
This is a multicenter, open-label, randomized phase III clinical trial evaluating perioperative treatment with sintilimab combined with chemotherapy in patients with locally advanced oral squamous cell carcinoma.

Despite standard treatment with surgery followed by postoperative radiotherapy or chemoradiotherapy, patients with locally advanced oral squamous cell carcinoma remain at high risk of recurrence or metastasis. Recent evidence, including results from the KEYNOTE-689 study, suggests…
conditions: Locally Advanced Oral Squamous Cell Carcinoma

interventions.name: Sintilimab plus nab-paclitaxel and platinum-based chemotherapy
Radical surgery combined with radiotherapy or chemoradiotherapy

related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Aged 18 to 75 years at the time of enrollment.
* ECOG Performance Status (PS) score of 0-1.
* Primary lesion pathologically confirmed as oral squamous cell carcinoma (OSCC), including tumors of the anterior two-thirds of the tongue, gingiva, buccal mucosa, floor of the mouth,…
related_trials.answers_question: Optimal IO sequencing: perioperative vs purely postoperative
related_trials.relevance_phrase: phase 3 perioperative chemo-IO in resected oral SCC

related_trials.brief_title: De-escalation Radiation Strategy After NAC in Combination With ICI in LAHNSCC
related_trials.brief_summary: This study is a single-arm exploratory study conducted in LAHNSCC. Eligible patients received two cycles of pembrolizumab immunotherapy in combination with albumin-bound paclitaxel and cisplatin induction chemotherapy, followed by definitive concurrent radiochemotherapy. Three months after the completion of radiotherapy, a follow-up examination was conducted, and salvage surgery or systemic therapy was performed as necessary based on the follow-up results.
conditions: Locally Advanced Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

1. ≥18 years old, with medical decision-making capacity
2. Signed informed consent
3. ECOG score of 0-1
4. Pathologically diagnosed with oral cancer, oropharyngeal cancer, laryngeal cancer, or hypopharyngeal cancer
5. Exclusion of distant metastasis
6. Clearly evaluable lesion…

For patients who have already undergone surgery and
